channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19598] TCU: Clean up files from the Trash Can
item.description:
A mechanism is needed to automatically clean up files from the trash can when the filesystem becomes full or the files in Trash Can are too old to expire the retain period.

  • For emergency case, potentially via MDS kernel thread to skip moving files into Trash Can (destroy the file directly), or delete the oldest pFID tree;
  • Userland policy engine can implement more sophisticated policy(age/space by user/projid, or file name, etc). It monitors the space/inode usage on the MDTs and OSTs periodically, once found the space is nearly full:
    • Scan MDT or OST devices for files in Trash Can via policy engine
    • Check TRASH and NOTRASH attributes on file (bot MDT and OST)
    • Check the original UID/GID/PROJID and deletion timestamp in "trusted.unrm" XATTR
    • Choose the candidate files or directories to be purged from Trash Can to free up space;

To improve the performance, we can use multiple threads to do file purge or improve the mpiFileUtils to scan and purge the files in Trash Can in parallel.
item.summary: TCU: Clean up files from the Trash Can

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
Here is the wiki page of ltrash_purge user guide:
https://wiki.whamcloud.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=431358480

"Emoly Liu <emoly@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/63165
Subject: LU-19598 utils: add a new API llapi_find_with_cb()
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: bd65175f326755f40352549215e7659b141a4784

"Emoly Liu <emoly@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/62946
Subject: LU-19598 utils: implement ltrash_purge tool
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: f4a79a6cbf2b9fcbe0f9e9cfed95ce4085d4c5b1

I think we should also add llite.*.trash_can_projid into the configuration file to account the space/inode usage by Trash Can.

I've already get this parameter in tcu_purge code. Should we set it in conf file?
I think we should also add llite.*.trash_can_projid into the configuration file to account the space/inode usage by Trash Can.

Here is ltcu_purge.conf sample to configure this userspace cleanup tool. To avoid the format issue to lpcc at https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54469, I chose to use yaml directly instead of json.

# Lustre mount point (required)                                                 
mount: /mnt/lustre                                                              
                                                                                
# Specific MDT index to clean (optional, default: all MDTs)                     
#mdt: MDT0000                                                                   
                                                                                
# MDT space watermark (percentage)                                              
# Start purging when MDT usage exceeds this threshold                           
high_usage_mdt: 90                                                          
                                                                                
# OST space watermark (percentage)                                              
# Start purging when OST usage exceeds this threshold                           
high_usage_ost: 90                                                         
                                                                                
# Age-based policy (in seconds)                                                 
# Start purging when the files are older than max_age
max_age: 2592000     # 30 days

# User/Group/Project filtering (optional)                                       
# Only purge files from these UIDs/GIDs/PROJIDs                                 
# Use comma-separated lists for multiple values                                 
#uid: 1000,1001,1002                                                            
#gid: 100,101                                                                   
#projid: 1,2,3                                                                  
                                                                                
# Scanning options                                                              
scan_interval: 60    # Seconds between scanning checks                                   
scan_threads: 4      # Number of parallel scanning threads

# Operational options                                                           
#dry_run: true       # Scan but do not delete files (for testing)

Since the files in the trash are not important to the user, we can delete the files when scanning, not like lpcc_purge in two-phase mode.

BTW, in the current design, we don't support any nodemap related trash cleanup.
We now have pfind in Lustre, so there should be no need to use mpifileutils unless we want to coordinate the scan across multiple clients
